title: US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Established
description: President Trump signed an executive order establishing a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, directing the Treasury to hold and not sell Bitcoin seized by the federal government. A separate digital asset stockpile was also created for other seized cryptocurrencies.

title: Trump Signs Pro-Crypto Executive Order
description: President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to promote digital asset development, protect the right to self-custody, and establish a Presidential Working Group on Digital Asset Markets. The order also rescinded prior restrictive crypto policy guidance.

title: Hong Kong Approves Spot Bitcoin & Ether ETFs
description: Hong Kong's SFC approved spot Bitcoin and Ether ETF listings for three issuers — ChinaAMC, Bosera, and HashKey. The move positions Hong Kong as the first major Asian financial hub to allow retail access to spot crypto ETFs.

title: SEC Approves 11 Spot Bitcoin ETFs
description: After a decade of rejections, the SEC approved spot Bitcoin ETF applications from BlackRock iShares, Fidelity, Ark 21Shares, Invesco, and seven others. Combined first-day trading volume exceeded $4.6 billion, making it one of the most successful ETF launches in history.

title: Grayscale Wins vs SEC in DC Circuit Court
description: The US Court of Appeals for the DC Circuit ruled in favour of Grayscale, finding the SEC's rejection of GBTC's conversion to a spot ETF was 'arbitrary and capricious.' The ruling was widely seen as a catalyst for the eventual spot ETF approvals in January 2024.

title: EU MiCA Regulation Enacted
description: The EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation was published in the Official Journal, creating the world's first comprehensive crypto regulatory framework for a major economic bloc. MiCA covers issuers of crypto-assets and CASPs, with full application from December 2024.

title: SEC Sues Coinbase and Binance
description: The SEC filed lawsuits against Coinbase (for operating as an unregistered securities exchange) and Binance (for broader securities violations including commingling funds). The dual enforcement actions intensified regulatory uncertainty for the entire crypto industry.

title: Ripple Partial Win vs SEC
description: Judge Analisa Torres ruled that XRP sold on secondary market exchanges did not constitute securities, handing Ripple a partial victory against the SEC. While institutional sales were found to be securities, the ruling offered a framework distinction that benefited the broader crypto market.

title: UAE Exempts Crypto from Capital Gains Tax
description: The UAE Federal Tax Authority confirmed that virtual assets and related transactions, including transfers and conversions, are exempt from VAT, with zero capital gains tax on crypto. The policy reinforces the UAE's position as a global crypto hub.

title: Australia Advances Crypto Exchange Licensing
description: ASIC issued updated guidance clarifying that crypto exchanges holding customer assets require an Australian Financial Services (AFS) licence. The framework aligns crypto custody with traditional financial services regulation.

title: Switzerland DLT Act Fully Operative
description: Switzerland's DLT Act — amending nine federal laws to accommodate distributed ledger technology — came into full effect, providing legal clarity for tokenised securities, DeFi, and digital asset custodians. Switzerland continues to lead Europe in crypto-friendly legislation.

title: Brazil Crypto Legal Framework Enacted
description: Brazil's Virtual Asset Service Provider Act (Law 14,478) took effect, establishing a licensing regime for crypto exchanges and a legal definition of virtual assets. The Central Bank of Brazil was designated as the primary regulator.

title: MAS Issues Stablecoin Regulatory Framework
description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore published its final stablecoin framework, requiring single-currency stablecoins pegged to SGD or G10 currencies to maintain full reserve backing and meet capital requirements. Non-compliant stablecoins may not call themselves MAS-regulated.

title: US Executive Order on Digital Assets
description: President Biden signed EO 14067 directing federal agencies to produce coordinated reports on crypto risks, benefits, and regulatory approaches. The order was broadly considered a signal that the US was moving toward a comprehensive crypto framework rather than an outright ban.

title: India Introduces 30% Crypto Tax
description: India's Finance Act introduced a 30% flat tax on income from virtual digital assets and a 1% TDS on transfers. The punitive tax structure — with no ability to offset losses across assets — was widely seen as discouraging domestic crypto activity.

title: CAR Adopts Bitcoin as Legal Tender
description: The Central African Republic became the second country in the world to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender, passing the bill unanimously in parliament. The move was described as a step toward economic sovereignty, though practical adoption remains limited by low internet penetration.

title: Germany Confirms Bitcoin Tax-Free After 1 Year
description: Germany's Federal Ministry of Finance formally confirmed that Bitcoin and Ether held for more than one year are exempt from capital gains tax for retail investors — regardless of whether coins were used in staking or lending. Germany remains the most favourable G7 country for long-term Bitcoin holders.

title: Canada Launches World's First Bitcoin ETF
description: Purpose Bitcoin ETF (BTCC) launched on the Toronto Stock Exchange, becoming the world's first exchange-listed spot Bitcoin ETF. It attracted over $400 million in assets in its first two days of trading.

title: El Salvador Makes Bitcoin Legal Tender
description: El Salvador became the first country in history to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender under the Bitcoin Law passed by the Legislative Assembly. The government launched the Chivo wallet and distributed $30 in BTC to citizens. The IMF expressed concerns over financial stability risks.

title: China Bans Bitcoin Mining
description: China's State Council Financial Stability and Development Committee called for a crackdown on Bitcoin mining and trading. Provincial governments including Inner Mongolia, Sichuan, and Xinjiang followed with immediate shutdowns, relocating roughly 50-60% of global hash rate abroad.

title: China Declares All Crypto Transactions Illegal
description: Ten Chinese agencies including the PBOC, NDRC, and MPS jointly declared all cryptocurrency transactions illegal and banned foreign exchanges from serving Chinese users. The blanket ban extended to DeFi and NFTs, making China's prohibition among the most comprehensive globally.

title: US Infrastructure Bill Expands Crypto Tax Reporting
description: The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act broadened the definition of 'broker' to include crypto custodians, exchanges, and potentially miners and validators. The expanded reporting requirements obligate brokers to issue 1099s for crypto transactions, raising compliance costs significantly.

title: Turkey Bans Crypto Payments
description: Turkey's central bank (CBRT) banned the use of cryptocurrencies and crypto-backed instruments as a means of payment for goods and services. The ban was attributed to risks including price volatility and potential use in illicit activity. Crypto trading on exchanges remained permitted.

title: SEC Rejects Bitwise and VanEck Spot ETF Filings
description: The SEC rejected multiple spot Bitcoin ETF proposals including those from Bitwise and VanEck, citing concerns about market manipulation and the lack of surveillance-sharing agreements with regulated Bitcoin markets. The rejections set off a multi-year campaign by asset managers to meet SEC standards.

title: Japan Recognises Bitcoin as Legal Property
description: Japan's Payment Services Act amendments came into effect, recognising Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies as legal property and creating one of the world's first formal crypto exchange licensing regimes under the FSA. The framework became a model for balanced crypto regulation globally.
